meta.nba_field_note: Casket purchase is one-time per family-event, not monthly. nba.monthly_sgd_equivalent here represents the per-event displaced casket-line spend baseline — what the buyer is comparing against (the casket line buried inside the traditional funeral bundle, or the alternative DTC option). Not a monthly recurring figure.

personas.name: Adult-child anti-pressure decision-maker
personas.icp: 35–54-year-old SG adult child of a recently-deceased parent, mobile-first, comparison-shops reflexively, has just been quoted S$8–15k by a parlour director for an opaque all-in package and is now researching the casket line specifically — often at 22:00 from a phone after the death-cert flow. A stranger names: a PMET in Bishan / Tampines / Pasir Ris with a dual-income household S$140–200k, the type who reads ST cost-of-dying coverage and posts on r/singapore.
pains: Just quoted S$8–15k all-in by parlour with no casket itemisation; smells the markup but doesn't know what fair price is
Parlour director threatened service withdrawal if family declines in-house casket — needs a counter-option fast
Mandai cremation slot already booked for ~36 hours from now; comparison-shopping window is hours, not days
Cannot bring this up at the family WhatsApp without seeming to negotiate over the parent's body
Existing parlour relationship inherited from extended family makes pushback socially costly
personas.current_workaround: Accept the parlour quote and quietly resent the markup for years; or DIY-research at midnight on mobile while the family sleeps, find Passage / 1Last / Direct Funeral via Google, place a 22:00 order with 24/7 helpline confirmation by 23:30.

nba.summary: The displaced spend is the casket line buried inside the traditional parlour S$8–15k bundle — triangulated mid-point S$2,500 for the casket alone (S$2,000–4,000 range per ST/CNA cost-of-dying coverage 2023–25). Passage at S$999 (Tranquil) saves ~S$1,500 on the casket line specifically.

personas.name: Direct-cremation route planner
personas.icp: Adult child or executor (sometimes a pre-arrangement senior themselves) who has decided on the no-wake / minimal-wake direct-cremation route — the casket is purely a logistics line, not a ceremonial centrepiece. Often non-religious or low-religiosity household. A stranger names: the 1Bereavement / Simplicity Funerals customer profile — someone who reads The Atlantic on death-care reform and intentionally rejects bundled-ceremony pricing.
pains: Direct-cremation 'package' from parlour still bundles a marked-up casket (S$1.8–3.2k total package, casket alone ~S$1.2–1.8k of that)
Wants to book the Mandai slot independently but the parlour controls the casket-to-Mandai logistics handoff
The minimal-wake / no-wake choice is already being questioned by extended family; price overrun on casket would re-open the debate
Eco / waste concerns — does not want imported tropical hardwood for a casket that will be cremated within 24 hours of delivery
Information-asymmetry pain: parlour knows the Mandai workflow, family does not
personas.current_workaround: Buy the parlour direct-cremation package and silently absorb the casket markup; or research-and-DIY: book Mandai slot via NEA portal directly, source Passage Serene S$499 separately, total spend S$799–1,500 vs S$1,800–3,200 parlour package. The DIY path requires confidence the family doesn't all share.

nba.summary: Direct-cremation parlour package mid-point S$2,500 (range S$1,800–3,200), of which the casket-line component is ~S$1,500. Passage Serene S$499 + DIY Mandai booking saves ~S$1,000 on casket alone, and lets the family book the slot themselves at NEA's transparent S$300 fee.

personas.name: Pre-arrangement senior + adult-child gateway
personas.icp: SG resident aged 60–80 actively planning their own arrangements (often after a peer's bereavement), usually in conversation with their adult child who is the operational contact. Wants fixed-price certainty, written commitment, and zero burden left to the family. A stranger names: an OCBC / Aviva 50+ retirement-planning client who has just attended an NTUC Health Active Ageing 'practical end-of-life' workshop.
pains: Has watched friends' families face S$8–15k bereavement bills and refuses to leave that uncertainty behind
Discomfort with the topic in family conversation; needs a calm, non-bereaved-time process to resolve it once
Wants the price LOCKED — refuses 'subject to availability' or 'prices may change' language
Needs adult-child to be the operational contact when the time comes (executor pattern)
Wary of 'funeral pre-need plans' because of historical insurance-product mis-selling complaints (CASE / MAS)
personas.current_workaround: Have the conversation, pick a parlour by extended-family default, leave a verbal preference but NO commitment — outcome is the same parlour quote at bereavement. Or: pre-pay an insurance-style 'funeral plan' that is actually a savings product with high lapse risk and ambiguous price-lock.

nba.summary: Comparable pre-need / pre-arrangement spend is the future-bundle midpoint S$2,500 casket-line (assuming current quotes hold to time of need, plus 0–4% inflation per year). Passage's Tranquil S$999 or Eternal S$1,299 with constitution-bound price-lock saves ~S$1,200–1,600 vs the future-bundle alternative AND removes the price-uncertainty drag.

personas.name: Social-worker-referred / compassionate case
personas.icp: ComCare recipient, deceased without next-of-kin, or compassionate-basis case routed via Passage's 8 welfare partners (Singapore Hospice Council, SASCO, Metta Welfare, AWWA, Ren Ci, Touch, Loving Heart, NTUC ElderCare). Decision happens via a social worker, not the family directly. A stranger names: a Toa Payoh / Bedok rental-flat resident on ComCare LTA whose only contact is an MSF case officer.
pains: Family unit is fragmented or absent; no next-of-kin to coordinate disposal logistics
MSF / ComCare coverage of bereavement is opaque and case-by-case; social worker cannot quote a price upfront
Time pressure: hospital morgue release window typically 48–72 hours; Mandai slot must be booked
Dignity-of-process anxiety — recipient does not want the absence of family to translate into the cheapest-possible disposal
Stigma resistance — refusing 'charity' framing while accepting structured assistance
personas.current_workaround: MSF case officer arranges minimum-viable disposal via parlour partner (often Casket Fairprice or basic-tier Singapore Casket bundle); ad-hoc charity supplements; or social worker refers to Passage's free-casket programme via the 8-partner network. FY2024: 31 of 47 Passage free caskets were social-worker-referred via this pathway.

nba.summary: The displaced 'spend' here is what the social-worker pathway would otherwise have to coordinate — minimum-viable parlour-supplied casket at ~S$500–800 effective (after parlour goodwill discount or MSF subsidy). Passage's free-casket programme (S$0, constitution-bound) replaces that scramble with a known, documented pathway through a named welfare partner.

pricing_models.name: Three-tier published flat pricing (Serene / Tranquil / Eternal)
pricing_models.rationale: Aligns with Agent 2's 'capture full value at first transaction' implication — this is a one-event purchase, no LTV multiplication via repeat. Single published price per SKU eliminates negotiation friction, removes pressure-tactic vector, and respects bereavement decision-time (hours, not days). All-in including delivery, venue positioning, engraved nameplate, and GST. The three tiers cover the WTP bands of all four personas without segment-leaking.

pricing_models.name: Bundled funeral package (parlour-style)
pricing_models.rationale: The model Passage exists to disrupt. Bundling casket inside an S$8–15k all-in funeral package with no casket-line itemisation is the source of the markup the brand calls out. Adopting this model would invalidate the brand's reason for existing.

pricing_models.name: Variable / negotiated pricing
pricing_models.rationale: Standard parlour practice — quote-on-call, customer can 'negotiate down' with effort. Penalises buyers who are uncomfortable negotiating during bereavement (most of them per ST 2024 coverage). Re-introduces the dignity-tax that Passage removes. Not viable for this brand.

pricing_models.name: Subscription / pre-need savings plan
pricing_models.rationale: Insurance-adjacent monthly-payment model where buyer pre-funds the casket over years. Looks attractive for pre-arrangement seniors but historically associated with mis-selling (CASE / MAS complaints), high lapse rates, and ambiguous price-lock. Bad fit for a trust-led DTC brand. A simple pre-paid lock-in (one-payment, constitution-bound price-honour) covers the same use-case without the reputational drag.

pricing_models.name: Free-via-binding-mechanism (programme tier alongside paid SKUs)
pricing_models.rationale: Passage's free-casket programme (47 in FY2024) is constitution-bound, not discretionary. Sits alongside the three paid SKUs as a structural pathway accessed via 8 welfare-org partners and direct social-worker referral. The binding mechanism (constitution + 30%-to-charity) makes this credible where parlour 'goodwill discounts' are not.

elasticity_heuristics.evidence: Anchor is the parlour bundle's hidden casket markup S$2,000–4,000 per ST 2024 / CNA 2023 coverage. Passage at S$999 (Tranquil) reads as ~50–75% saving on the casket line specifically — well above the threshold where the buyer feels they've 'won' the comparison. Push-back from extended family ('isn't it disrespectful to go cheap?') is neutralised by serif-noble SKU naming + dignity-pricing language. Walks above ~S$1,500 only if the dignity narrative breaks down. The S$999 / S$1,299 price points are well-calibrated — not a price-elasticity battleground.

By definition, this segment has decided to minimise total bereavement spend; every dollar saved on the casket is a dollar redirected to columbarium niche, post-disposal celebration-of-life, or simply held back from the bereavement category entirely. Walks above ~S$900. Serene S$499 is precisely calibrated to capture this segment as a lead-gen wedge — the loss-leader aspect is structural, not promotional. Family does NOT comparison-shop within Passage's tier ladder; they pick Serene unless aesthetic / ceremonial defaults force Tranquil.

Anchor is the future cost of NOT pre-arranging — i.e. leaving family to face S$8–15k bundle quotes while bereaved. Will pay above headline if the lock-in is constitution-bound (legally enforceable price-honour). OCBC SG 2026 Retirement Pulse: pre-need buyers consistently rate 'price certainty' above 'lowest absolute price' by 2.4× margin. Tranquil S$999 / Eternal S$1,299 land well within the WTP band; Serene S$499 reads as 'not enough dignity' for this persona's family-anxiety frame. Will pay UP if the brand can certify multi-decade price-honour.

Programme tier — not a price-elasticity question. The structural question is access: does the social worker know the pathway, can they trigger it without case-by-case approval, does the recipient retain dignity through the process. FY2024 outcome: 31 of 47 free caskets via 8 welfare-org partners — pathway is working but discoverability remains the constraint, not price.

recommended_tiers.name: Serene
what_in: Casket — paulownia softwood, FSC-certified, locally assembled in SG under PWM
Venue positioning at delivery (HDB void deck, condo function room, parlour, or home)
9% GST included in the published price
24/7 helpline access from order to delivery (one named contact)
30% net profit allocated to charity (binding via constitution)
what_excluded: Wake setup, embalming, hearse, religious rites — Passage sells caskets only
Mandai cremation slot booking — family books direct via NEA portal at S$300
Columbarium niche — handled via NEA / private operators separately
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: S$499 is one-fifth of the casket-line markup hidden inside the typical parlour bundle (S$2,500 mid-point) — enough below to make the maths obvious, enough above to retain dignity language without slipping into discount framing.

recommended_tiers.name: Tranquil
what_in: Casket — sustainably-sourced hardwood (acacia or rubberwood), FSC-certified, satin-finish lining
Upgraded interior fabric and pillow finish
Optional family viewing arrangement at delivery (15 minutes)
Free-casket programme cross-subsidy contribution noted in receipt
what_excluded: Wake / embalming / rites — Passage sells caskets only
Mahogany or premium hardwood (upgrade to Eternal)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: S$999 sits at less than half the parlour casket-line markup midpoint (S$2,500) and is the centre of all four personas' WTP bands — the 'dignified default' choice for the anti-pressure adult-child decision-maker.

recommended_tiers.name: Eternal
what_in: Casket — premium hardwood (FSC-certified mahogany or oak), satin-lined, hand-finished detailing
Documented timber provenance (FSC chain-of-custody certificate per casket)
Pre-need price-lock option (constitution-bound — price honoured indefinitely from purchase date)
Named relationship contact (single point of contact for adult child / executor)
what_excluded: Imported exotic hardwood beyond FSC catalogue (out of scope by brand policy)
Wake / embalming / rites — Passage sells caskets only
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: S$1,299 is roughly half the parlour casket-line markup midpoint and within the upper-stretch WTP for pre-arrangement seniors and eco-values buyers — fixed price + constitution-bound lock-in is the 'no surprise for the family' guarantee.

grants.name: Not applicable — bereavement / casket purchase falls outside SG SME / consumer-tech grant scope
grants.eligibility: PSG, EDG, WDG(JR+), SFEC and other Enterprise Singapore / WSG / IMDA grants cover SME productivity tools, workforce transformation, and consumer-tech adoption — not consumer-facing bereavement purchases. ComCare and MSF welfare assistance can subsidise specific hardship cases, but at the household level not the vendor level.

grants.name: ComCare bereavement grants (household-level, not vendor-routed)
grants.eligibility: MSF ComCare offers case-by-case bereavement assistance up to ~S$1,300 per qualifying household for a minimum-viable funeral. Disbursed to the family, not the casket vendor. Passage's free-casket programme is the operational complement — 31 of 47 FY2024 casket-grants were social-worker-routed without ComCare cash flow needing to touch Passage at all.
